hook.text: In the 1990s, builders started applying Toyota's lean principles to construction.

buildup.text: Construction was plagued by 57% waste — materials, waiting, rework, and overproduction.

discovery.text: Lean construction uses pull planning: workers plan together, tasks start only when needed.

twist.text: The Last Planner System reduced project delays by 30% on early adopter sites.

climax.text: An industry stuck in the 19th century started using 20th century ideas — and they worked.

punchline.text: Toyota's factory ideas rebuilt the construction site.

quiz.question: What percentage of waste was typical in construction before lean methods?
